items.title: Butters found guilty of abusing umpire as Port claims 'glaring hole in the evidence'

children.content: Port Adelaide star Zak Butters is found guilty of umpire abuse at the AFL Tribunal, despite strongly denying allegations he asked umpire Nick Foot "how much are they paying you?".

articles.title: Butters asked 'why did you pay that?' Koch says

children.content: Port Adelaide chairman David Koch says the AFL club will back Zak Butters "to the hilt" amid dispute over what was said to an umpire during Sunday night's loss against St Kilda.

image.altText: AFL player Zak Butters at a media conference.

articles.title: How the AFL's gamble with conflict of interest concerns didn't pay off

children.content: The AFL gambled that concerns over a conflict of interest with one of its umpires wouldn't be an issue, but that call has come back to bite them — and it's not the umpire's fault.

image.altText: An umpire smiles while being interviewed during a press conference.

items.title: 'Fierce and defiant' disability advocate remembered after drowning

children.content: Ellen Fraser-Barbour is being remembered for her tireless advocacy and love of nature after her death at the age of 35.

image.altText: A disability advocate with her friend.

items.title: Residents near cement plant raise concerns about more plastic being burnt for fuel

children.content: Residents living near a large cement plant in Adelaide are concerned about the owner wanting to burn more plastic as fuel to replace natural gas.

image.altText: aerial image of a manufacturing plant with river to the right and cloudy sky in background

items.title: Blow to cartography in Australia as The Map Shop prepares to close

children.content: One of Australia's few remaining cartography shops is about to close in Adelaide, prompting questions about where people can buy the critical documents, particularly those travelling in remote areas where mobile coverage is limited.

image.altText: Man stands in front of shelf of maps

items.title: Man says he was acting in 'self-defence' when he kicked a dog twice in the head

children.content: A northern suburbs man says he had been bitten and was acting in self-defence when he kicked a dog that was tethered to the tray of a ute in a Bunnings car park.

image.altText: Maya was allegedly kicked to the head while in a Bunnings car park

items.title: ASIC investigating 'individuals' within Sanjeev Gupta's collapsed empire

children.content: After years of financial red flags, corporate watchdog ASIC confirms it has multiple investigations underway into Sanjeev Gupta's troubled empire, following multiple collapses that have left hundreds of workers facing an uncertain future.

items.title: Rate rises, spending cuts and cost blowouts: Councils squeezed by fuel crisis

children.content: Some South Australian councils may hike rates above inflation this year as they grapple with cost blowouts, spending cuts and project delays caused by the fuel crisis.

image.altText: two diggers in a fenced off area with flattened dirt next to a road with cars passing by

items.title: Qantas cancels flights between Adelaide and Mount Gambier amid fuel shortages

children.content: Qantas will no longer fly to South Australia's largest regional city, citing fuel shortages and declining demand for flights to Mount Gambier.

image.altText: Qantas Mount Gambier

items.title: Ex-One Nation candidate wants Hanson 'held accountable' for rehiring rapist ex-staffer

children.content: Two former One Nation candidates have accused party leader Pauline Hanson and chief of staff James Ashby of dismissing concerns raised internally about the convicted rapist and former campaign director Sean Black.

image.altText: A composite of two images: one of a middle-aged woman, the other a middle-aged man.

items.title: One Nation approaches independent MPs as it makes the case to be SA's opposition

children.content: One Nation SA leader Cory Bernardi has approached independent MPs to make the case for his party — instead of the Liberals — to be recognised as the state's official opposition.

image.altText: composite of Matt Schultz, Lou Nicholson and Geoff Brock

items.title: Reptile remains believed to be a crocodile found dumped on side of road

children.content: Reptile remains — which are believed to be from a crocodile — have been collected by authorities after they were found dumped on the side of an Adelaide road.

image.altText: A crocodile in brown water looking straight on at the camera close-up like a portrait.

items.title: Independent supermarkets struggle to keep prices down as Iran war hurts

children.content: Suppliers are charging more to deliver groceries to supermarkets as fuel prices increase, putting pressure on smaller retailers.

image.altText: Fresh vegetables on three black shelves with price stickers in front of the items.

items.title: Judgement day for high-stakes battle of iron ore dynasties

children.content: Gina Rinehart, the wealthiest person in Australia, expects a judgement to be handed down today in an explosive court battle with the family of her father's former business partner over iron ore royalties.

gallery.altText: Gina Rinehart smiling and chatting to reporters at the Bush Summit in Perth.
Red dirt at the side of the road with a sign that says 'Hope Downs mine 30km'
Bianca wears a brown top and white skirt as she walks next to John who wears a blue suit

thumbnail.altText: Gina Rinehart smiling and chatting to reporters at the Bush Summit in Perth.
Red dirt at the side of the road with a sign that says 'Hope Downs mine 30km'
Bianca wears a brown top and white skirt as she walks next to John who wears a blue suit

items.title: Collard handed monster ban over 'entirely unacceptable' homophobic slur

children.content: St Kilda forward Lance Collard is suspended after a marathon AFL Tribunal hearing with a deliberation of over 90 minutes.

image.altText: Lance Collard stands with both arms in the air during a St Kilda vs West Coast Eagles AFL match in 2025.

items.title: Palestinian groups take Australia to court over Israel arms exports

children.content: A group of Palestinian human rights organisations launch legal action against the defence minister seeking transparency over Australia's arms exports to Israel.

image.altText: A grey fighter jet close up in teh sky.

items.title: Prince Harry and Meghan kick off Australia visit with stop at Melbourne hospital

children.content: The Duke and Duchess of Sussex have begun their multi-day visit to Australia in Melbourne, meeting with patients at a children's hospital.

articles.title: As the Sussexes return to Australia, 'unusual' commercial engagements are raising eyebrows

children.content: Harry and Meghan will be travelling under their titles of Duke and Duchess of Sussex, and will appear at two major, ticketed events as well as making the rounds of various charities and causes.

image.altText: prince harry and his wife Meghan leaving a cathedral in the UK smiling and happy.

gallery.altText: Meghan Markle talking to a young girl
Prince Harry greets a boy as he visits the Royal Children's Hospital in Melbourne.

thumbnail.altText: Meghan Markle talking to a young girl
Prince Harry greets a boy as he visits the Royal Children's Hospital in Melbourne.

items.title: Harvey Weinstein to face retrial on New York rape charge

children.content: Former Hollywood magnate Harvey Weinstein is again going on trial on a rape charge in New York City. He will remain imprisoned for other offences regardless of the verdict.

image.altText: A bald man sits in court wearing a suit

items.title: Fuel excise cut 'too focused on politics', analysts say as concerns grow

children.content: Some energy analysts are questioning the federal government's temporary tax cuts on fuel, warning that lower prices may work against Australia's long-term needs and goals.

image.altText: Five red petrol pumps in a bowser; one with a red handle, then a blue, a green, a dark blue and a black handle.

items.title: WA children to get needle-free flu vaccine

children.content: Western Australia is offering free needle-free flu vaccines for children in a bid to curb declining inoculation rates and prevent another deadly influenza season.

image.altText: A young boy receives a nasal spray flu vaccine

items.title: Kitty Flanagan and Anne Edmonds create comedy chaos in new show

children.content: Plus Nicole Kidman is an Aussie wrestler in Margo's Got Money Troubles, Baby Reindeer's Richard Gadd returns, and the reboots keep on coming with Malcolm in the Middle.

image.altText: A woman in a red and blue costume leans on the bars of a wrestling rink

items.title: INXS is out, Phil Collins in as Rock & Roll Hall of Fame 2026 inductees announced

children.content: INXS has missed out on landing a place in the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame with 2026 inductees including Phil Collins, Queen Latifah and Fela Kuti.

gallery.altText: Phil Collins singing into a microphone, mouth wide open, arm up, pink and blue lights in background

thumbnail.altText: Phil Collins singing into a microphone, mouth wide open, arm up, pink and blue lights in background

items.title: How I've learnt to embrace the new normal of only having one breast

children.content: I decided to keep my healthy breast, opting against reconstruction. It means I'm now what's known in the community as a uniboober.

image.altText: Aarthi with her dog during chemo treatment

items.title: Inquiry told school to lose students if $1.5-billion waste incinerator built

children.content: A school in the middle of a $22-million master plan for its growing enrolment numbers is worried the buildings will not be needed if a waste to energy plant is approved in the town.

image.altText: A man in a suit standing on a shopping street

items.title: Dramatic dash cam vision of dangerous overtaking prompts safety call

children.content: A 21-year-old woman was charged with reckless driving after dash cam video allegedly caught her overtaking a triple road train in an "unsafe" manner.

image.altText: A still image of a car almost colliding with a truck, captured by a dash cam.

gallery.altText: A still image of a car almost colliding with a truck, captured by a dash cam.

thumbnail.altText: A still image of a car almost colliding with a truck, captured by a dash cam.

items.title: Man who sent threatening letters was 'drinking himself to sleep'

children.content: A man who sent threatening letters to a Sydney mosque was "drinking himself to sleep" and "fell into some type of internet rabbit hole", a court hears.

image.altText: lakemba mosque

items.title: Killers chauffeured interstate after Gavin Preston underworld hit, prosecutors allege

children.content: Prosecutors allege two men were chauffeured back to NSW after conducting a contract killing of the Melbourne underworld figure in 2023.

image.altText: A pale photo of an unsmiling, bald man between two blurred figures.

items.title: Australia's largest cattle feedlot on the market as grain-fed beef surges

children.content: Mort & Co, which fattens hundreds of thousands of cattle on grain before being slaughtered for beef, puts the majority of its assets on the market.

image.altText: Aerial photo of a large feed lot.

items.title: CFMEU breached industrial laws at least 25 times in 14 years, inquiry hears

children.content: Former Australian Building and Contruction Commission head Nigel Hadgkiss has given evidence at the Commission of Inquiry into the CFMEU in Queensland.

image.altText: A red flag with CFMEU on it in white text.

items.title: Stagflation is a 'central banker's nightmare', says RBA deputy governor

children.content: RBA deputy governor Andrew Hauser says stagflation is a "central banker's nightmare" and the coming months will be challenging for Australia.

image.altText: Close up of Andrew Hauser with flags behind him

items.title: WA government says it has secured 4 million litres of diesel

children.content: Four million litres of diesel is set to arrive in Western Australia "in the coming weeks" as part of a strategic stockpile to address acute, localised fuel shortages across the state.

image.altText: Fuel prices advertised at more than $2.50 a litre at Ampol petrol station.

items.title: Safety alert issued amid explosion of illegal peptide use

children.content: The medicines regulator issues a health alert about the risks of using unregulated peptides amid a surge in the use of potentially unsafe injectables.

image.altText: A close up of a syringe and needle

items.title: 'Difficult trade-offs' needed in renewable transition, minister condedes

children.content: The New South Wales energy minister has met with disgruntled landowners affected by the New England Renewable Energy Zone.

image.altText: Three people stand in a line looking into the camera and frowning.

items.title: Rescuers free young whale after 24-hour stranding ordeal

children.content: A whale stranded on a sandbar in Wallis Lake on the NSW mid north coast has been freed by marine animal rescue teams.

gallery.altText: Four people in Sea World wetsuits and another person in life jacket and helmet standing in water near a stranded whale

thumbnail.altText: Four people in Sea World wetsuits and another person in life jacket and helmet standing in water near a stranded whale

items.title: Colombia approves cull of up to 80 hippos

children.content: Colombian officials have authorised a plan to cull dozens of hippos, years after notorious drug lord Pablo Escobar brought in the first ones.

image.altText: Five hippos are floating in water. Most are half submerged in the lagoon.

items.title: Fiji Police are circling an Australian wanted over four-tonne meth import

children.content: Key insiders from Fiji's largest-ever meth bust say the drug import had alarming links to organised crime in Australia.

image.altText: A man in a blue singlet and a baseball cap drinking a coconut through a straw with gym equipment behind him.
